projects.name: Feast
projects.short_description: Feast is an open source feature store that delivers structured data to AI and LLM applications at high scale during training and inference.
projects.description: Feast helps teams operate production ML systems at scale by allowing them to define, manage, validate, and serve features for production AI/ML. Feast's feature store is composed of two foundational components: (1) an offline store for historical feature extraction used in model training and (2) an online store for serving features at low-latency in production systems and applications.  It is a configurable operational data system that re-uses existing infrastructure to manage and serve machine learning features to real-time models.

form.question: How to support
form.response:
- Contribute features, bug fixes, or performance improvements and bench-marking (especially around offline/online store integrations, scalability, and infra).
- Improve documentation.
- Share feedback from production deployments.
- Help review PRs (this is very valuable).

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: Feast is an open-source feature store that sits at the center of modern ML systems. It standardizes how features are defined, stored, and served - bridging offline training and online inference. It enables teams to reuse features, ensure consistency between training and serving, and scale ML systems using existing data infrastructure.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: **Design for contributors, not just users.** Clean abstractions and good docs matter more than clever code if you want a project to grow.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response:
Because ML infrastructure shouldn’t be reinvented in every company.
Feast solves a real, recurring problem and building it in the open means better ideas, broader adoption, and real impact across teams and industries.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
form.response:
Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger
(It’s basically what every feature pipeline is trying to become.)

form.question: Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: Started by fixing a small bug… At some point, you stop fixing bugs and start redesigning the system.

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
form.response: 🧩 (You’re constantly fitting together pieces from users, infra, and ideas - while new pieces keep showing up.)
